.codex\credibility-redesign\p1_diff_gate_verdict.stderr.txt:654:docs/credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md:148:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**, computed by this EXECUTABLE invariant (Codex iter-2 P1-2): group every supporting row by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)`; for each origin cluster L3 designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** (the syndication root / earliest independent publication; the other members are derivative copies — genuinely-INDEPENDENT corroboration has different content and forms its OWN cluster, it does not join an existing one). `cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical origin)`; copy/derivative members are attributed for disclosure ("N copies → 1 origin") but contribute ZERO to the mass. The claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **No row-level term, no averaging, no max-over-copies.** A row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id` only because L3 flagged it a copy of that cluster's canonical origin — so it adds NOTHING to the mass **even if its own `authority_score` is higher than the canonical origin's** (a high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content would form a new cluster). **Binding invariant (mathematically closed, Codex iter-3):** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY authority of the copier, whenever the added row joins an existing origin cluster. This requires **L3 to emit a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation, and membership** (NOT merely an `independent_origin_count` scalar) and L4 to emit `claim_cluster_id`, both BEFORE L5. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.
.codex\credibility-redesign\p1_diff_gate_verdict.stderr.txt:655:docs/credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md:150:- **Clinical source-type veto (Codex iter-1 P1-3 — wired, not a slogan):** a clinical-domain claim requires ≥1 **independent clinical-tier source (T1/T2 clinical)** in its origin-clustered support; if absent, Phase 6 emits a **`not_weight_adequate` / `clinical_source_type_veto`** signal REGARDLESS of news/commercial weight-mass; **Phase 7 (L6) consumes that signal to render ABSTAIN/fringe** (the rendering is Phase 7, not here). They are not the same gate and must not be conflated.

Phase 4a emitted-contract made explicit (`origin_cluster_id` + canonical designation + copy flag, with a P6-groupability test); drb_72 Verification bullet made audit-conditional; Phase 6 clinical-veto bullet emits a signal (rendering moved to Phase 7); Phase 10 adds the `SENTINEL_ORIGINS` (`plan_sufficiency_gate.py`) update + fallback test. **Iter-4 (REQUEST_CHANGES) addressed:** replaced every remaining normative `max(authority_score)` with `authority_score(canonical_origin)` (§3 L3/L5 rows + Phase 4a scope) so the formula is consistent everywhere; tightened the clinical-veto wording (Phase 6 owns the `clinical_source_type_veto` emission, Phase 7 only renders); removed the duplicate `Files:` bullets in Phase 4a + Phase 7. **Iter-5: APPROVE** (Codex binding plan-gate, 0 P0 / 0 P1, convergence=accept_remaining). One P2 residual — keep the per-claim clinical source-type veto distinct from the corpus clinical adequacy floor — noted inline in Phase 6 for the implementation brief.
9:
10:**Binding acceptance gate (READ FIRST).** The source doc does not stop at §4's six layers. Its §6 ends with:
11:> `proceed_to_plan: yes, CONDITIONAL on the plan incorporating 6a (no overstatement), 6b (added systems/benchmarks), and ALL of 6c (the 8 required layers).`
12:§6 is **authoritative over §4** where they differ. This plan therefore delivers against **all 8 of §6c**, honors §6a (no overstatement), and pulls §6b's systems/benchmarks into the test strategy. A clean six-layer plan that ignores §6c would *feel* complete and still fail this gate.
13:
14:---
15:
16:## 1. GOAL + NON-NEGOTIABLES
17:
18:### 1.1 Goal
19:
20:Turn POLARIS from a **count-and-tier-threshold** sourcing pipeline into a **credibility-WEIGHTED, independence-aware, per-claim-disclosed** one — the union that no deployed system offers (§3): a disclosed credibility weight that (a) is domain-conditional, (b) discounts non-independent corroboration *before* aggregation, and (c) actually drives composition and is shown per claim. We extend POLARIS's existing hard substrate — per-sentence `[#ev:id:start-end]` tokens + `strict_verify` + zero-verified-abort — from *provenance disclosure* to *provenance + weight + independent-origin-count + certainty disclosure*.
21:
22:### 1.2 Non-negotiables (these override every design choice below)
23:
24:1. **Faithfulness gates are preserved, never weakened.** The four gates stay intact and any verifier work is **additive-only**:
25:   - **strict_verify** (`provenance_generator.py:1156` `verify_sentence_provenance`) — six per-sentence checks (evidence-pool membership, span bounds, numeric match, ≥2 content-word overlap, trial-name, entailment). New work *adds* checks on top (§6c-7); it never relaxes one of the six.
26:   - **4-role / two-family segregation** (`check_family_segregation`, enforced e.g. `semantic_conflict_detector.py:346`) — untouched. Generator and evaluator stay cross-lineage.
27:   - **provenance token grammar** `[#ev:id:start-end]` — unchanged. New fields are *side-outputs* on `SentenceVerification`, never inputs to the verdict.
28:   - **corpus_approval_gate** (`corpus_approval_gate.py`, FX-05) — stays an **independent downstream backstop**. Weight-based adequacy does NOT bypass it; both gates run sequentially.
29:2. **No silent downgrade (LAW II + operator directive 2026-06-04).** Every new layer ships behind a flag, **default-OFF byte-identical**, fail-loud when its inputs are missing. Capability caps are surfaced and per-cap approved, never silently applied.
30:3. **Domain-aware, not a global hierarchy (§4 ISSUE 4 — "there is no universal evidence hierarchy").** Clinical weights RCT+peer-review+RoB; econ/policy weights design-validity over venue (the Nobel-validated shift — a strong NBER natural experiment must not rank below a weak peer-reviewed cross-section); qualitative weights coherence/adequacy. Domain is detected off the existing `scope_templates`.
31:4. **Sovereign + capture-resistant.** `authority_model.py` is already host-name-free, data-driven (LAW VI — "ZERO host names in code"). The credibility prior stays **multi-signal, self-computed, and OVERRIDABLE by the claim-level verifier** — no single external rater (NewsGuard/MBFC/Ad Fontes) hardwired as ground truth (the FTC-capture risk in §4 ISSUE 3).
32:5. **Clinical-safety weighting invariant (task §6 + §6c-8).** Web/news *volume* must never outweigh the **absence of clinical evidence**; medical/health misinformation is disclosed as **low-weight / fringe**, explicitly NOT normalized as an equal "side." Honest failure mode is **ABSTAIN** ("contested; no independent consensus"), never a fabricated balanced verdict.
33:6. **Weight beats count, and independence is collapsed before the vote** — the through-line of the whole source doc.
34:
35:---
36:
37:## 2. TARGET ARCHITECTURE — the 8 layers mapped onto our concrete files
38:
39:The source doc frames a six-stage flow `retrieve → score → independence-collapse → aggregate → compose → disclose`. §6c **extends** this with three net-new layers (temporal/supersession, claim-graph-before-voting, retrieval dissent-recall) and four cross-cutting strengtheners (article+claim scoring, independence-beyond-TFIDF, calibration/audit, additive verifier). The table below is the full §6c-aware target; **REUSE** = existing code we re-wire, **BUILD** = genuinely new.
40:
41:| # | Layer (flow stage) | What it does | REUSE (existing file:anchor) | BUILD (new) |
42:|---|---|---|---|---|
43:| L0 | **Retrieve broadly + dissent-recall** (§6c-6) | All-source-type retrieval; *actively seek* the best minority-side / contrary evidence; stratify by source-type | `live_retriever.py` retrieval lanes; subquery-reserve diversity pass `evidence_selector.py:552-660` (`_reserve_subqueries:602`); domain soft-cap `:661-704` (`_apply_domain_cap`); selection-time passes `:1513-1542` | dissent-recall sub-query expansion + source-type stratification quota (net-new, upstream of scoring) |
44:| L1 | **Score — two-axis, domain-conditional, article+claim** (§4 L1 + §6c-1) | Reliability × Relevance, primary axis chosen by domain; article-level (author/venue/method/funding/date/corrections) AND claim-specific relevance, not just host prior | `authority_model.py` signals A–E (scalar blend); `tier_classifier.py:135-142` additive fields `authority_score`/`corroboration_count`/`authority_confidence`; `scope_templates/` for domain | second **relevance** axis; domain-conditional rubric selector; article-level + claim-level decomposition (real BUILD — current blend is one field-agnostic scalar) |
45:| L2 | **Temporal / supersession** (§6c-2) | Downgrade stale-but-authoritative evidence (old guidelines, superseded regs/datasets, retracted/corrected) even when the source is high-authority | Signal E recency `recency.py` / `authority_model.py:138-145`; `is_retracted` in journal sidecar `journal_only_filter.py:237` | supersession rules (guideline/reg/dataset version), retraction → **hard** penalty surfaced per claim |
46:| L3 | **Independence-collapse — beyond near-duplicate** (§4 L2 + §6c-4) | Collapse content-copying / syndication / common-ownership / common-funding / shared-authorship / citation-chain / paraphrase clusters to ~1 origin BEFORE any weighted vote | `corroboration.py` eTLD+1 PSL host-collapse (`count_independent_hosts`, `registrable_domain`); `finding_dedup.py` already clusters by finding + counts independent registrable-domains (`dedup_by_finding`, host-based) | TF-IDF cosine ≥0.85 **content-copy detector** + ownership/funding/authorship signals on top of the host primitive (real BUILD — current collapse is host-based only) |
47:| L4 | **Claim-graph BEFORE aggregation** (§6c-3, NEW sub-layer) | atomic-claim extraction → normalization → stance clustering → contradiction/refutation detection → span entailment, so equivalent claims are clustered before any vote | `finding_dedup.py` numeric-finding clustering (seed, clinical-numeric only); `semantic_conflict_detector.py` + `qualitative_conflict_detector.py` + numeric `contradiction_detector.py` (stance/contradiction seeds); entailment path in `verify_sentence_provenance` | field-agnostic atomic-claim extractor + normalization + stance-cluster graph (real BUILD — finding_dedup is numeric-only and inert on non-clinical) |
48:| L5 | **Aggregate by weight, auditable non-averaging** (§4 L3) | Weighted Majority Voting over post-collapse independent origins; inverse-variance for quantitative; GRADE "lowest-among-critical" for multi-part certainty | `plan_sufficiency_gate.py` already uses `authority_score` as a numeric GATE (`:50`, `:301`) | replace COUNT aggregation (`plan_sufficiency_gate.py:313` `covered_count >= target`; journal `DEFAULT_MIN_DISTINCT_JOURNALS=12` `journal_only_filter.py:531`) with **origin-cluster weight-mass** (one weight per independent-origin cluster; copied rows cannot inflate mass — Phase 6 invariant) |
49:| L6 | **Contested-topic composition — weight-and-disclose-with-forewarning** (§4 L4 + §6c-8 multi-position) | Consensus side at high weight AND minority **attributed** at low weight, with explicit **forewarning** (the N=887 finding: forewarning works, outnumbering does not). Multi-position, not binary. Medical misinfo = low-weight/fringe. ABSTAIN over fabricated balance | cross-lineage multi-judge agreement via the EXISTING 4-role adjudication — integration point `roles/role_pipeline.py` + `honest_sweep_integration.py` (REUSE, do NOT build a parallel adjudication path); existing refuse/unresolved behavior; retraction flag | forewarning composition rule + multi-position (>2 side) disclosure policy + clinical-fringe down-weight |
50:| L7 | **Per-claim disclosure** (§4 L5 + §6c-1 claim-level) | Each verified sentence carries {span-verdict, credibility_weight, independent_origin_count, certainty_label}; rendered in bibliography; "Proof Replay" SUPPORTS-not-EXISTS UX | `SentenceVerification` additive-field precedent (`soft_warnings` `:427`, `judge_error` `:433`); `resolve_provenance_to_citations` `:1881` render point | four optional fields + bibliography render + certainty-label scoring fn |
51:| L8 | **Additive verifier strengthening** (§6c-7) | NLI/QA entailment + unit/table/quantity checks + contradiction-sensitive verification **ON TOP of** strict_verify's six (never weakening) | existing NLI advisory path; `semantic_conflict_detector.py`; numeric checks in `verify_sentence_provenance` | wire the advisory NLI/contradiction checks as additive fail-closed gates (flag-gated, ON-mode only) |
52:| Lx | **Calibration + audit** (§6c-5) + **adversarial benchmark** (§4 L6) | Brier/ECE reliability curves on the weights; blinded per-claim faithfulness eval; ablations per layer; SourceBench/DeepTRACE/ReportBench suites | existing manifest telemetry; `outputs/audits/` §-1.1 harness | calibration metric script + adversarial vax fixture + benchmark harness (test infra, not pipeline) |
53:
54:**Reuse vs build, in one honest sentence each:**
55:- **`authority_model.py`** blends A–E into ONE field-agnostic scalar — the second (relevance) axis + domain conditioning is a real BUILD, not a rewire.
56:- **`finding_dedup.py` / `corroboration.py`** give us the independent-origin PRIMITIVE, but **host-based** only; §6c-4 content-copy detection is a BUILD on top.
57:- **`SentenceVerification` + `resolve_provenance_to_citations`** are the disclosure substrate — additive fields, lowest-risk first phase.
58:- **`plan_sufficiency_gate.py`** already treats `authority_score` as a numeric gate — converting its COUNT aggregation to WEIGHT-MASS is a small, surgical re-wire.

62:## 3. GAP TABLE — per layer: HAVE vs NEEDED vs the file:line change
63:
64:| Layer | HAVE | NEEDED | Specific change (file:line) |
65:|---|---|---|---|
66:| L0 dissent-recall | breadth retrieval + diversity passes (`evidence_selector.py:532-704` subquery-reserve+domain-cap, `:1513-1542`) | active minority-side retrieval + source-type stratification | new sub-query expansion lane in `live_retriever.py` retrieval orchestration; a `dissent_seed` origin (mirror the existing `agentic_seed`/`deepener_seed` sentinel lanes in `plan_sufficiency_gate.py:53-59`) |
67:| L1 two-axis score | scalar `authority_score` blend A–E (`authority_model.py:159-170`); domain in `scope_templates` | **relevance** axis + domain-conditional rubric + article/claim decomposition | add `relevance_score` to `AuthorityResult` (`source_class.py:75`); new `credibility_prior.py` selecting rubric off detected domain; emit on rows in `tier_classifier.py:2035-2038` |
68:| L2 temporal/supersession | Signal E recency; `is_retracted` (`journal_only_filter.py:237`) | supersession downgrade + retraction hard penalty per claim | new `supersession.py` rule module; consume in L1 score as a multiplier; surface as a `soft_warning` + certainty downgrade |
69:| L3 independence-collapse | host eTLD+1 collapse (`corroboration.py:58-64`); finding-clustering (`finding_dedup.py:121`) | **content-copy** TF-IDF ≥0.85 + ownership/funding/authorship | new `independence_collapse.py`; emits a stable `origin_cluster_id` + membership per row (NOT just `independent_origin_count`); runs BEFORE L5 (which takes `authority_score(canonical_origin)` once per origin cluster; derivative copies contribute zero) |
70:| L4 claim-graph | numeric finding clusters (`finding_dedup.py`); conflict detectors | field-agnostic atomic-claim graph + stance clustering | new `claim_graph.py`; reuses conflict detectors for the contradiction/refutation edges; gates L5 (vote only over clustered-equivalent claims) |
71:| L5 weighted aggregate | COUNT thresholds (`plan_sufficiency_gate.py:313`); journal COUNT floor (`journal_only_filter.py:531,596`) | origin-cluster weight-mass = Σ over origin clusters of `authority_score(canonical_origin)` (one mass per `origin_cluster_id` = its canonical origin's authority; derivative copies contribute zero; NO row-level term, NO max-over-copies — Phase 6 invariant) | replace `covered_count >= target` with `weight_mass >= weight_target` (`plan_sufficiency_gate.py:312-317`); add `assess_journal_only_adequacy` weight path (`journal_only_filter.py:543-607`); consumes L3 `origin_cluster_id` |
72:| L6 forewarning compose | refuse/unresolved; 4-role agreement | weight-and-disclose-with-forewarning; multi-position; clinical-fringe | new composition rule in the generator's contested-claim path; forewarning string injected via `soft_warnings` channel |
73:| L7 per-claim disclose | `SentenceVerification` fields; bibliography render | 4 new optional fields + render | add `span_verdict`/`credibility_weight`/`independent_origin_count`/`certainty_label` to `SentenceVerification` (`provenance_generator.py:419-433`); extend bibliography dict (`:1897-1903`) |
74:| L8 additive verifier | strict_verify six checks; advisory NLI | NLI/unit/contradiction as additive fail-closed gates | new `additive_verifiers.py` invoked AFTER the six checks in `verify_sentence_provenance`; ON-mode flag-gated, never relaxes the six |
75:| Lx calibration/bench | manifest telemetry; §-1.1 harness | Brier/ECE + ablations + vax fixture + suites | new `tests/fixtures/credibility/` + `scripts/credibility_calibration.py`; benchmark harness for §6b suites |
76:
77:**§6a corrections honored (no overstatement):** AuthorityBench is cited only as an authority-PERCEPTION benchmark, NOT a volume-vulnerability benchmark. DeepTRACE Perplexity numbers are framed "low minimum-cover / weak grounding," not "94.5% decorative." RA-RAG is the aggregation primitive AFTER independence-collapse, not a solution to it. The "79%→17% tool-call collapse" stat is replaced by the supportable "39–77% factual accuracy / ~42% average fact-check drop." CAG is EMNLP 2024 and its labels are relevance/timeliness, not source authority. RAGRank is inspiration (CTI PoC), not a validated web echo detector. Community-Notes/Elicit stats are flagged as needing their own primary citations before being treated as settled.

79:---
80:
81:## 4. PHASED DELIVERY
82:
83:**Phasing spine (the codebase's own proven pattern).** Every new layer ships behind a flag, **default-OFF byte-identical**, activated only in the Gate-B slate — exactly how `journal_only_active(protocol)`, `judge_error`, and `quantified_models=None` already behave. This single discipline yields: ≤200-LOC self-contained Codex-gated phases, "no silent downgrade," AND a trivially-true faithfulness-safety argument per phase (OFF ⇒ byte-identical output + identical rendered artifacts). The disclosure SCHEMA ships first as inert plumbing; independence-collapse populates it later (per the JSON map's own warning: do not deploy *populated* per-claim disclosure until independence-collapse exists).
84:
85:Each phase below = one GitHub Issue, one brief → Codex APPROVE → one diff → Codex APPROVE on the Red-Team checklist (the standard §3.0 triple). Phases are ordered by dependency and risk; the claim-graph (L4) and two-axis rescore (L1) are explicitly decomposed because neither fits in a single ≤200-LOC unit.
86:
87:> **Honest sizing note:** L4 claim-graph and L1 two-axis are NOT one ≤200-LOC phase each. They are split (P4a/P4b, P2a/P2b). Where a phase legitimately exceeds 200 LOC, it requests a documented §3.0 LOC exemption in the brief rather than being split into incoherent halves.
88:
89:### Phase 1 — Disclosure schema (inert plumbing). [L7, lowest risk]
90:- **Scope:** Add four optional fields to `SentenceVerification` (dataclass-only inert plumbing). The bibliography render is NOT changed here — the four keys are emitted ONLY when the credibility-disclosure flag is ON (Phase 8), so OFF stays byte-identical (Codex iter-2 P2: extra empty keys would break byte-identity). NO population yet (defaults: `span_verdict=""`, `credibility_weight=None`, `independent_origin_count=None`, `certainty_label=""`).
91:- **Files:** `provenance_generator.py:419-433` (dataclass only). The bibliography dict (`:1897-1903`) is touched in Phase 8 (flag-gated render), NOT here.
92:- **Change:** add the four fields with safe defaults; NO bibliography-render change (preserves OFF byte-identity); token grammar + `is_verified` untouched.
93:- **Offline tests:** backward-compat (old callers read new objects); OFF byte-identity of rendered report + bibliography; the six strict_verify checks unchanged.
94:- **Verification:** diff shows only additive fields; smoke run produces byte-identical `report.md` with empty new fields.
95:- **Faithfulness-safety:** new fields are side-outputs, never inputs to the verdict. OFF = byte-identical. Trivially safe.
96:
97:### Phase 2a — Relevance axis + domain rubric selector (score, part 1). [L1]
98:- **Scope:** Add a second `relevance_score` axis to `AuthorityResult`; a `credibility_prior.py` that selects the primary axis off the detected domain (clinical/econ-policy/qualitative) from `scope_templates`. Flag-gated; OFF ⇒ legacy scalar unchanged.
99:- **Files:** `source_class.py:75` (AuthorityResult), new `authority/credibility_prior.py`, `tier_classifier.py:2035-2038` (emit).
100:- **Change:** compute relevance (directness-to-question) and conditionally select the reliability rubric; emit both axes additively.
101:- **Offline tests:** econ natural-experiment (NBER) must out-score a weak peer-reviewed cross-section **only when the flag is ON**; OFF byte-identity; thin-signal → honest LOW confidence (never fabricated HIGH).
102:- **Verification:** diff is additive-fields-only; OFF smoke produces byte-identical `report.md` + manifest; the two-axis selection-flip is reproducible on the unit fixture.
103:- **Faithfulness-safety:** SELECTION-side only — `verify_sentence_provenance` and the six checks are untouched; scoring changes WHICH rows are billed, not how a claim verifies its span; corpus_approval backstop still runs. OFF = byte-identical.
104:
105:### Phase 2b — Article-level + claim-level decomposition (score, part 2). [L1 + §6c-1]
106:- **Scope:** Decompose the prior into author/venue/method/funding/date/corrections (article) AND claim-specific relevance, disclosed sub-criteria NewsGuard-style; overridable by the verifier.
107:- **Files:** `credibility_prior.py`, `authority_model.py` signal plumbing.
108:- **Offline tests:** decomposed sub-scores sum/compose correctly; a high-venue but stale/retracted article scores lower than a fresh direct one; capture-resistance test (no single external rater can flip a verdict alone).
109:- **Verification:** additive-fields diff; OFF byte-identity smoke; decomposition unit fixture reproduces the sub-scores.
110:- **Faithfulness-safety:** SELECTION-side only — verifier and the six checks untouched; the decomposed prior is overridable by the claim-level verifier (capture defense); corpus_approval still runs. OFF = byte-identical.
111:
112:### Phase 3 — Temporal / supersession + retraction hard penalty. [L2, §6c-2]
113:- **Scope:** `supersession.py` downgrade rules for superseded guidelines/regs/datasets; retraction → hard penalty + per-claim surfacing.
114:- **Files:** new `authority/supersession.py`; consume in `credibility_prior.py`; surface via `soft_warnings` + certainty downgrade.
115:- **Offline tests:** an old-guideline source is downgraded vs the current one; a retracted source is hard-penalized and the penalty surfaces per claim.
116:- **Verification:** additive-fields diff; OFF byte-identity smoke; supersession + retraction rules reproduce on the unit fixture.
117:- **Faithfulness-safety:** SELECTION/disclosure-side only — verifier and the six checks untouched; downgrade surfaces as a `soft_warning` (additive channel, existing precedent), never drops a verified span. OFF = byte-identical.
118:
119:### Phase 4a — Content-copy / syndication detector (independence, part 1). [L3, §6c-4]
120:- **Scope:** `independence_collapse.py` — TF-IDF cosine ≥0.85 near-duplicate clustering over the retrieved corpus, layered on the existing host-collapse primitive. Curated acceptable-mirror handling (arXiv/SSRN/PMC) to bound false positives. **Emits a stable `origin_cluster_id` + canonical-origin designation + cluster membership per row** (L5 takes one `authority_score(canonical_origin)` mass per cluster — derivative copies contribute zero — Codex iter-3), not merely a scalar `independent_origin_count`.
121:- **Files:** new `synthesis/independence_collapse.py`; reuse `corroboration.py:58-64` + `finding_dedup.py` host primitive.
122:- **Offline tests:** 50 near-verbatim copies of one press release collapse to `independent_origin_count≈1`; legitimate parallel reporting / arXiv mirror does NOT over-collapse (false-positive bound).
123:- **Verification:** new-module diff (no edits to verifier or selection paths yet). **Phase 4a MUST emit, per row: a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation + cluster membership, a copy/derivative flag, AND `independent_origin_count`** — with a test asserting P6 can group by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)` and that copies are flagged (so P6's origin-cluster mass is computable); OFF byte-identity smoke; collapse + false-positive fixtures reproduce.
124:- **Faithfulness-safety:** pure scoring side-output; touches no faithfulness gate; collapse NEVER drops a source, only de-weights duplicated corroboration. OFF = byte-identical.
125:
126:### Phase 4b — Ownership / funding / authorship / citation-chain signals (independence, part 2). [L3, §6c-4]
127:- **Scope:** Extend the collapse beyond content-copy to common-ownership/funding/shared-authorship/citation-chain/paraphrase clusters.
128:- **Files:** `synthesis/independence_collapse.py`.
129:- **Offline tests:** common-owner network collapses even without verbatim copy; independent same-finding sources keep `independent_origin_count > 1`.
130:- **Verification:** additive-signal diff; OFF byte-identity smoke; ownership/funding fixtures reproduce.
131:- **Faithfulness-safety:** pure scoring side-output; no faithfulness gate touched; never drops a source. OFF = byte-identical.
132:
133:### Phase 5a — Atomic-claim extractor + normalization (claim-graph, part 1). [L4, §6c-3]
134:- **Scope:** Field-agnostic atomic-claim extraction + normalization (generalizes `finding_dedup.py`'s clinical-numeric-only extractor, which is inert on non-clinical numerics).
135:- **Files:** new `synthesis/claim_graph.py`; reuse `contradiction_detector.extract_numeric_claims` as one extractor among several.
136:- **Offline tests:** non-clinical numerics (GDP, emissions) now produce claims (current `finding_dedup` returns nothing); conservative-singleton safety preserved (never over-merge distinct claims).
137:- **Verification:** new-module diff; OFF byte-identity smoke; the conservative-singleton safety rule (never over-merge distinct claims) reproduces on the `finding_dedup` clinical fixtures.
138:- **Faithfulness-safety:** pre-aggregation analysis side-output; touches no faithfulness gate; default on ambiguity is "keep separate" (no distinct-claim loss). OFF = byte-identical.
139:
140:### Phase 5b — Stance clustering + contradiction edges (claim-graph, part 2). [L4, §6c-3]
141:- **Scope:** Stance clustering + contradiction/refutation/entailment edges over the normalized claims, reusing the three conflict detectors.
142:- **Files:** `synthesis/claim_graph.py`; reuse `semantic_conflict_detector.py`/`qualitative_conflict_detector.py`/`contradiction_detector.py`.
143:- **Offline tests:** equivalent claims cluster; contradictory claims form an edge (recall-first on the refutes class per §4 L4 — over-detect, fail loud); the graph gates L5 (vote only over clustered-equivalents).
144:- **Verification:** new-edge-builder diff; OFF byte-identity smoke; stance + contradiction fixtures reproduce; the three detectors' existing fail-open/recall-first behavior is unchanged.
145:- **Faithfulness-safety:** analysis side-output; the conflict detectors keep their existing safety contracts (semantic fail-open never fabricates a conflict; qualitative escalates-to-review never silent-drops). OFF = byte-identical.
146:
147:### Phase 6 — Weighted aggregation (replace count with weight-mass). [L5]
148:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**, computed by this EXECUTABLE invariant (Codex iter-2 P1-2): group every supporting row by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)`; for each origin cluster L3 designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** (the syndication root / earliest independent publication; the other members are derivative copies — genuinely-INDEPENDENT corroboration has different content and forms its OWN cluster, it does not join an existing one). `cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical origin)`; copy/derivative members are attributed for disclosure ("N copies → 1 origin") but contribute ZERO to the mass. The claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **No row-level term, no averaging, no max-over-copies.** A row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id` only because L3 flagged it a copy of that cluster's canonical origin — so it adds NOTHING to the mass **even if its own `authority_score` is higher than the canonical origin's** (a high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content would form a new cluster). **Binding invariant (mathematically closed, Codex iter-3):** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY authority of the copier, whenever the added row joins an existing origin cluster. This requires **L3 to emit a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation, and membership** (NOT merely an `independent_origin_count` scalar) and L4 to emit `claim_cluster_id`, both BEFORE L5. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.
149:- **Files:** `plan_sufficiency_gate.py:312-317` (verdict math), `journal_only_filter.py:543-607` (`assess_journal_only_adequacy` weight path; demote `DEFAULT_MIN_DISTINCT_JOURNALS=12` count-floor to a weight-floor when journal-only is NOT protocol-pinned).
150:- **Clinical source-type veto (Codex iter-1 P1-3 — wired, not a slogan):** a clinical-domain claim requires ≥1 **independent clinical-tier source (T1/T2 clinical)** in its origin-clustered support; if absent, Phase 6 emits a **`not_weight_adequate` / `clinical_source_type_veto`** signal REGARDLESS of news/commercial weight-mass; **Phase 7 (L6) consumes that signal to render ABSTAIN/fringe** (the rendering is Phase 7, not here). docs\credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md:148:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**, computed by this EXECUTABLE invariant (Codex iter-2 P1-2): group every supporting row by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)`; for each origin cluster L3 designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** (the syndication root / earliest independent publication; the other members are derivative copies — genuinely-INDEPENDENT corroboration has different content and forms its OWN cluster, it does not join an existing one). `cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical origin)`; copy/derivative members are attributed for disclosure ("N copies → 1 origin") but contribute ZERO to the mass. The claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **No row-level term, no averaging, no max-over-copies.** A row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id` only because L3 flagged it a copy of that cluster's canonical origin — so it adds NOTHING to the mass **even if its own `authority_score` is higher than the canonical origin's** (a high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content would form a new cluster). **Binding invariant (mathematically closed, Codex iter-3):** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY authority of the copier, whenever the added row joins an existing origin cluster. This requires **L3 to emit a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation, and membership** (NOT merely an `independent_origin_count` scalar) and L4 to emit `claim_cluster_id`, both BEFORE L5. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.

.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:1:# I-cred-004b (#1161) — undated-cluster copy-invariance spec decision + missing-evidence_id stable id — BRIEF for Codex
.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:10:This is a DESIGN/SPEC decision, not a diff. You raised this at the P4 (#1153) iter-5 cap. I need your ruling on the undated-cluster policy before Phase 6 (#1155) wires `cluster_mass`.
.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:14:`src/polaris_graph/synthesis/independence_collapse.py` `_order_key`: for an ALL-undated cluster the canonical is the LOWEST-`authority_score` member. This makes `cluster_mass = authority(canonical)`:
.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:15:- **Inflation direction FULLY blocked** — a higher-authority copy can never become canonical, so `weight_mass(rows + higher_auth_copy) == weight_mass(rows)`. ✓ (the vax-defense property)
.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:16:- **But NOT strict equality for a LOWER-authority copy** — adding a copy whose authority is below the current min makes it the new canonical and LOWERS the mass. The plan §148 states `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY copier authority.
.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:20:## Why strict equality is unachievable for undated differing-authority clusters
.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:24:For an ALL-undated cluster of near-duplicate content with DIFFERING authority, there is no date and no guaranteed-monotonic identity to mark "the original." Any member-authority-based mass function that is invariant to adding an arbitrary-authority element is either a constant (throws away the signal) or ignores the new element (requires a stable original-identity we do not have). So "strict equality for ANY copier authority" cannot hold for undated clusters without either (a) requiring dates, or (b) not collapsing differing-authority undated near-dups at all.
.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:28:**1. Undated-cluster policy = conservative MIN authority (the current behaviour), documented as deliberate.** Rationale: it blocks the only DANGEROUS direction (inflation) exactly, and a lower-authority copy LOWERING the represented authority is honest information — the same content also appearing on a less-authoritative source means the claim is not exclusive to high-authority origins, so a lower mass is arguably MORE faithful, not a bug. Amend the plan to state: strict equality holds for DATED clusters; undated clusters use conservative-min (equality for copies of authority ≥ min; safe monotonic-lowering otherwise; inflation impossible for any copier authority).
.codex\credibility-redesign\approve_comment.md:5:**Convergence trail (5-iter binding gate):** iter1 = 6 findings → iter2 = 3 → iter3 = 1 real P1 + 4 → iter4 = formula-consistency → **iter5 = APPROVE**. The load-bearing catch was the **origin-cluster weight-mass copy-invariance**: the mass is the authority of each cluster's *canonical origin*, so copied/syndicated rows — of ANY authority — cannot inflate a side. That is the mechanism that makes the vax-case defense real.

I reproduced: base canonical `origin`; after adding a high-authority undated copy, canonical becomes `copy_undated_high`. This directly breaks the required 'copied row cannot change canonical origin / mass' property."

.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter2.stderr.txt:1216:148:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**, computed by this EXECUTABLE invariant (Codex iter-2 P1-2): group every supporting row by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)`; for each origin cluster L3 designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** (the syndication root / earliest independent publication; the other members are derivative copies — genuinely-INDEPENDENT corroboration has different content and forms its OWN cluster, it does not join an existing one). `cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical origin)`; copy/derivative members are attributed for disclosure ("N copies → 1 origin") but contribute ZERO to the mass. The claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **No row-level term, no averaging, no max-over-copies.** A row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id` only because L3 flagged it a copy of that cluster's canonical origin — so it adds NOTHING to the mass **even if its own `authority_score` is higher than the canonical origin's** (a high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content would form a new cluster). **Binding invariant (mathematically closed, Codex iter-3):** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY authority of the copier, whenever the added row joins an existing origin cluster. This requires **L3 to emit a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation, and membership** (NOT merely an `independent_origin_count` scalar) and L4 to emit `claim_cluster_id`, both BEFORE L5. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.

.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter2.stderr.txt:2300:  - "src/polaris_graph/synthesis/independence_collapse.py:197: `_order_key` treats any non-empty date string as valid and compares it lexically, despite the spec saying publication dates are used only when present and parseable. A derivative copy with `published_date: '01/01/2099'` can sort before canonical `published_date: '2024-01-01'` and become canonical, violating the P4 copy-invariance/canonical-origin invariant. The tests only cover ISO dates and undated rows."
.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter3.txt:9:  - "Fix invalid ISO date parsing so malformed copied rows are treated as undated and cannot steal canonical origin status."
.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter4.txt:5:  - "P1: Undated prepended copies can still steal the canonical origin. `_order_key` falls back to row index for undated rows, and canonical selection is `min(... _order_key)`, so an undated high-authority copy inserted before an undated seed becomes canonical and changes `origin_cluster_id`/future `cluster_mass`. This violates the P4 copy-invariance invariant. See `src/polaris_graph/synthesis/independence_collapse.py:210` and `:401`; existing tests cover prepended copies only when the seed has a valid date (`tests/polaris_graph/test_independence_collapse.py:334`, `:405`)."

Phase 4a emitted-contract made explicit (`origin_cluster_id` + canonical designation + copy flag, with a P6-groupability test); drb_72 Verification bullet made audit-conditional; Phase 6 clinical-veto bullet emits a signal (rendering moved to Phase 7); Phase 10 adds the `SENTINEL_ORIGINS` (`plan_sufficiency_gate.py`) update + fallback test. **Iter-4 (REQUEST_CHANGES) addressed:** replaced every remaining normative `max(authority_score)` with `authority_score(canonical_origin)` (§3 L3/L5 rows + Phase 4a scope) so the formula is consistent everywhere; tightened the clinical-veto wording (Phase 6 owns the `clinical_source_type_veto` emission, Phase 7 only renders); removed the duplicate `Files:` bullets in Phase 4a + Phase 7. **Iter-5: APPROVE** (Codex binding plan-gate, 0 P0 / 0 P1, convergence=accept_remaining). One P2 residual — keep the per-claim clinical source-type veto distinct from the corpus clinical adequacy floor — noted inline in Phase 6 for the implementation brief.

.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter3.stderr.txt:1279:120:- **Scope:** `independence_collapse.py` — TF-IDF cosine ≥0.85 near-duplicate clustering over the retrieved corpus, layered on the existing host-collapse primitive. Curated acceptable-mirror handling (arXiv/SSRN/PMC) to bound false positives. **Emits a stable `origin_cluster_id` + canonical-origin designation + cluster membership per row** (L5 takes one `authority_score(canonical_origin)` mass per cluster — derivative copies contribute zero — Codex iter-3), not merely a scalar `independent_origin_count`.
.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter3.stderr.txt:1285:148:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**, computed by this EXECUTABLE invariant (Codex iter-2 P1-2): group every supporting row by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)`; for each origin cluster L3 designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** (the syndication root / earliest independent publication; the other members are derivative copies — genuinely-INDEPENDENT corroboration has different content and forms its OWN cluster, it does not join an existing one). `cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical origin)`; copy/derivative members are attributed for disclosure ("N copies → 1 origin") but contribute ZERO to the mass. The claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **No row-level term, no averaging, no max-over-copies.** A row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id` only because L3 flagged it a copy of that cluster's canonical origin — so it adds NOTHING to the mass **even if its own `authority_score` is higher than the canonical origin's** (a high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content would form a new cluster). **Binding invariant (mathematically closed, Codex iter-3):** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY authority of the copier, whenever the added row joins an existing origin cluster. This requires **L3 to emit a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation, and membership** (NOT merely an `independent_origin_count` scalar) and L4 to emit `claim_cluster_id`, both BEFORE L5. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.
.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter3.stderr.txt:1286:151:- **Offline tests:** **the vax adversarial test** (N independent-LOOKING-but-copied low-cred sources vs few high-cred — naive count flips to the false majority, weight+collapse picks the high-cred side); **copy-invariance fixture (Codex iter-3): a copier whose own `authority_score` is HIGHER than the cluster's canonical origin joins that origin cluster → `weight_mass` is UNCHANGED (the copier is derivative, excluded from the cluster mass)**; drb_72 regression **(CONDITIONAL on the journal-only audit, Decision #4)** — assert weight-floor passage for drb_72 ONLY if the audit confirms journal-only was an implementation choice, not a protocol requirement; otherwise assert on a non-protocol-pinned fixture (a protocol-pinned `source_restriction: journal_only` keeps its hard T1+T2 filter); corpus_approval still blocks material-deviation auto-approve even when weight-adequate (AuthorizedSweep still required); **clinical veto test (adequacy-level, Codex iter-2)** — a clinical claim with only news/commercial support is **NOT weight-adequate** / the clinical source-type veto fires (the T1/T2 clinical-tier floor is empty), even at high weight-mass. The ABSTAIN/fringe *rendering* is asserted in Phase 7 (L6 composition), not here.

.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter5.txt:5:  - "P1: `src/polaris_graph/synthesis/independence_collapse.py:219-238,406-418` violates the binding copy-invariance invariant in `docs/credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md:148`. For all-undated clusters, `_order_key` chooses the lowest `authority_score` as canonical, so adding a lower-authority copied row that joins an existing cluster changes the canonical, changes `origin_cluster_id`, and lowers `cluster_mass`. The spec requires `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for any copier authority, and a copied row cannot change canonical origin. Current tests only cover higher-authority copies, so the green smoke misses this."

The claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **No row-level term, no averaging, no max-over-copies.** A row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id` only because L3 flagged it a copy of that cluster's canonical origin — so it adds NOTHING to the mass **even if its own `authority_score` is higher than the canonical origin's** (a high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content would form a new cluster). **Binding invariant (mathematically closed, Codex iter-3):** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY authority of the copier, whenever the added row joins an existing origin cluster. This requires **L3 to emit a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation, and membership** (NOT merely an `independent_origin_count` scalar) and L4 to emit `claim_cluster_id`, both BEFORE L5. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.
.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter5.stderr.txt:2619: 151: - **Offline tests:** **the vax adversarial test** (N independent-LOOKING-but-copied low-cred sources vs few high-cred — naive count flips to the false majority, weight+collapse picks the high-cred side); **copy-invariance fixture (Codex iter-3): a copier whose own `authority_score` is HIGHER than the cluster's canonical origin joins that origin cluster → `weight_mass` is UNCHANGED (the copier is derivative, excluded from the cluster mass)**; drb_72 regression **(CONDITIONAL on the journal-only audit, Decision #4)** — assert weight-floor passage for drb_72 ONLY if the audit confirms journal-only was an implementation choice, not a protocol requirement; otherwise assert on a non-protocol-pinned fixture (a protocol-pinned `source_restriction: journal_only` keeps its hard T1+T2 filter); corpus_approval still blocks material-deviation auto-approve even when weight-adequate (AuthorizedSweep still required); **clinical veto test (adequacy-level, Codex iter-2)** — a clinical claim with only news/commercial support is **NOT weight-adequate** / the clinical source-type veto fires (the T1/T2 clinical-tier floor is empty), even at high weight-mass. The ABSTAIN/fringe *rendering* is asserted in Phase 7 (L6 composition), not here.

.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter5.stderr.txt:2818:  - "P1: `src/polaris_graph/synthesis/independence_collapse.py:219-238,406-418` violates the binding copy-invariance invariant in `docs/credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md:148`. For all-undated clusters, `_order_key` chooses the lowest `authority_score` as canonical, so adding a lower-authority copied row that joins an existing cluster changes the canonical, changes `origin_cluster_id`, and lowers `cluster_mass`. The spec requires `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for any copier authority, and a copied row cannot change canonical origin. Current tests only cover higher-authority copies, so the green smoke misses this."
.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter5.stderr.txt:2824:  - "Restore strict copy-invariance: a copied row joining a cluster must not change canonical identity, `origin_cluster_id`, or `cluster_mass`, including lower-authority undated copies."

.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter4.stderr.txt:4055:  - "P1: Undated prepended copies can still steal the canonical origin. `_order_key` falls back to row index for undated rows, and canonical selection is `min(... _order_key)`, so an undated high-authority copy inserted before an undated seed becomes canonical and changes `origin_cluster_id`/future `cluster_mass`. This violates the P4 copy-invariance invariant. See `src/polaris_graph/synthesis/independence_collapse.py:210` and `:401`; existing tests cover prepended copies only when the seed has a valid date (`tests/polaris_graph/test_independence_collapse.py:334`, `:405`)."

.codex\credibility-redesign\p5_diff_gate_verdict.stderr.txt:995:148:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**, computed by this EXECUTABLE invariant (Codex iter-2 P1-2): group every supporting row by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)`; for each origin cluster L3 designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** (the syndication root / earliest independent publication; the other members are derivative copies — genuinely-INDEPENDENT corroboration has different content and forms its OWN cluster, it does not join an existing one). `cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical origin)`; copy/derivative members are attributed for disclosure ("N copies → 1 origin") but contribute ZERO to the mass. The claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **No row-level term, no averaging, no max-over-copies.** A row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id` only because L3 flagged it a copy of that cluster's canonical origin — so it adds NOTHING to the mass **even if its own `authority_score` is higher than the canonical origin's** (a high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content would form a new cluster). **Binding invariant (mathematically closed, Codex iter-3):** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY authority of the copier, whenever the added row joins an existing origin cluster. This requires **L3 to emit a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation, and membership** (NOT merely an `independent_origin_count` scalar) and L4 to emit `claim_cluster_id`, both BEFORE L5. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.

.codex\credibility-redesign\plan_gate_input_iter2.md:175:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**. The claim-side weight-mass is summed over **independent-origin CLUSTERS** (from L3/L4), exactly ONE weight per cluster (its max-authority representative) — NOT per row. **Binding invariant (Codex iter-1 P1-2):** adding a copied / non-independent row to an existing origin cluster MUST NOT increase the claim-side weight-mass — `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` when the copy joins an existing cluster; equivalently each row contributes `authority_score / cluster_size`. Without this, copied rows each carrying `independent_origin_count=1` re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Requires L3 (origin clustering) + L4 (claim clustering) to run BEFORE L5; aggregation is over (claim-cluster × origin-cluster) cells. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.

.codex\credibility-redesign\plan_gate_input_iter4.md:174:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**, computed by this EXECUTABLE invariant (Codex iter-2 P1-2): group every supporting row by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)`; for each origin cluster L3 designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** (the syndication root / earliest independent publication; the other members are derivative copies — genuinely-INDEPENDENT corroboration has different content and forms its OWN cluster, it does not join an existing one). `cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical origin)`; copy/derivative members are attributed for disclosure ("N copies → 1 origin") but contribute ZERO to the mass. The claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **No row-level term, no averaging, no max-over-copies.** A row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id` only because L3 flagged it a copy of that cluster's canonical origin — so it adds NOTHING to the mass **even if its own `authority_score` is higher than the canonical origin's** (a high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content would form a new cluster). **Binding invariant (mathematically closed, Codex iter-3):** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY authority of the copier, whenever the added row joins an existing origin cluster. This requires **L3 to emit a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation, and membership** (NOT merely an `independent_origin_count` scalar) and L4 to emit `claim_cluster_id`, both BEFORE L5. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.

.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:13:# I-cred-004b (#1161) — undated-cluster copy-invariance spec decision + missing-evidence_id stable id — BRIEF for Codex
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:22:This is a DESIGN/SPEC decision, not a diff. You raised this at the P4 (#1153) iter-5 cap. I need your ruling on the undated-cluster policy before Phase 6 (#1155) wires `cluster_mass`.
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:26:`src/polaris_graph/synthesis/independence_collapse.py` `_order_key`: for an ALL-undated cluster the canonical is the LOWEST-`authority_score` member. This makes `cluster_mass = authority(canonical)`:
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:27:- **Inflation direction FULLY blocked** — a higher-authority copy can never become canonical, so `weight_mass(rows + higher_auth_copy) == weight_mass(rows)`. ✓ (the vax-defense property)
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:28:- **But NOT strict equality for a LOWER-authority copy** — adding a copy whose authority is below the current min makes it the new canonical and LOWERS the mass. The plan §148 states `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY copier authority.
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:32:## Why strict equality is unachievable for undated differing-authority clusters
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:36:For an ALL-undated cluster of near-duplicate content with DIFFERING authority, there is no date and no guaranteed-monotonic identity to mark "the original." Any member-authority-based mass function that is invariant to adding an arbitrary-authority element is either a constant (throws away the signal) or ignores the new element (requires a stable original-identity we do not have). So "strict equality for ANY copier authority" cannot hold for undated clusters without either (a) requiring dates, or (b) not collapsing differing-authority undated near-dups at all.
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:40:**1. Undated-cluster policy = conservative MIN authority (the current behaviour), documented as deliberate.** Rationale: it blocks the only DANGEROUS direction (inflation) exactly, and a lower-authority copy LOWERING the represented authority is honest information — the same content also appearing on a less-authoritative source means the claim is not exclusive to high-authority origins, so a lower mass is arguably MORE faithful, not a bug. Amend the plan to state: strict equality holds for DATED clusters; undated clusters use conservative-min (equality for copies of authority ≥ min; safe monotonic-lowering otherwise; inflation impossible for any copier authority).
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:65:I’ll ground the ruling in the current implementation and spec text before answering, especially the canonical ordering and any documented invariants around `cluster_mass`/`evidence_id`.
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:67:"C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe" -Command 'rg -n "cluster_mass|weight_mass|evidence_id|origin_cluster_id|independence|copy-invariance|copy invariance|undated|authority" -S .' in C:\POLARIS
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:113:(``cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical_origin)``, copies contribute

.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:2315:.\docs\credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md:120:- **Scope:** `independence_collapse.py` — TF-IDF cosine ≥0.85 near-duplicate clustering over the retrieved corpus, layered on the existing host-collapse primitive. .codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:2320:.\docs\credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md:148:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**, computed by this EXECUTABLE invariant (Codex iter-2 P1-2): group every supporting row by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)`; for each origin cluster L3 designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** (the syndication root / earliest independent publication; the other members are derivative copies — genuinely-INDEPENDENT corroboration has different content and forms its OWN cluster, it does not join an existing one). `cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical origin)`; copy/derivative members are attributed for disclosure ("N copies → 1 origin") but contribute ZERO to the mass. The claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **No row-level term, no averaging, no max-over-copies.** A row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id` only because L3 flagged it a copy of that cluster's canonical origin — so it adds NOTHING to the mass **even if its own `authority_score` is higher than the canonical origin's** (a high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content would form a new cluster). **Binding invariant (mathematically closed, Codex iter-3):** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY authority of the copier, whenever the added row joins an existing origin cluster. This requires **L3 to emit a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation, and membership** (NOT merely an `independent_origin_count` scalar) and L4 to emit `claim_cluster_id`, both BEFORE L5. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:2321:.\docs\credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md:151:- **Offline tests:** **the vax adversarial test** (N independent-LOOKING-but-copied low-cred sources vs few high-cred — naive count flips to the false majority, weight+collapse picks the high-cred side); **copy-invariance fixture (Codex iter-3): a copier whose own `authority_score` is HIGHER than the cluster's canonical origin joins that origin cluster → `weight_mass` is UNCHANGED (the copier is derivative, excluded from the cluster mass)**; drb_72 regression **(CONDITIONAL on the journal-only audit, Decision #4)** — assert weight-floor passage for drb_72 ONLY if the audit confirms journal-only was an implementation choice, not a protocol requirement; otherwise assert on a non-protocol-pinned fixture (a protocol-pinned `source_restriction: journal_only` keeps its hard T1+T2 filter); corpus_approval still blocks material-deviation auto-approve even when weight-adequate (AuthorizedSweep still required); **clinical veto test (adequacy-level, Codex iter-2)** — a clinical claim with only news/commercial support is **NOT weight-adequate** / the clinical source-type veto fires (the T1/T2 clinical-tier floor is empty), even at high weight-mass. The ABSTAIN/fringe *rendering* is asserted in Phase 7 (L6 composition), not here.

.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:6969:.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:32:## Why strict equality is unachievable for undated differing-authority clusters
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:6971:.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:36:For an ALL-undated cluster of near-duplicate content with DIFFERING authority, there is no date and no guaranteed-monotonic identity to mark "the original." Any member-authority-based mass function that is invariant to adding an arbitrary-authority element is either a constant (throws away the signal) or ignores the new element (requires a stable original-identity we do not have). So "strict equality for ANY copier authority" cannot hold for undated clusters without either (a) requiring dates, or (b) not collapsing differing-authority undated near-dups at all.
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:6972:.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:40:**1. Undated-cluster policy = conservative MIN authority (the current behaviour), documented as deliberate.** Rationale: it blocks the only DANGEROUS direction (inflation) exactly, and a lower-authority copy LOWERING the represented authority is honest information — the same content also appearing on a less-authoritative source means the claim is not exclusive to high-authority origins, so a lower mass is arguably MORE faithful, not a bug. Amend the plan to state: strict equality holds for DATED clusters; undated clusters use conservative-min (equality for copies of authority ≥ min; safe monotonic-lowering otherwise; inflation impossible for any copier authority).

.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:8762:.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:2315:.\docs\credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md:120:- **Scope:** `independence_collapse.py` — TF-IDF cosine ≥0.85 near-duplicate clustering over the retrieved corpus, layered on the existing host-collapse primitive. .codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:8764:.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:2320:.\docs\credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md:148:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**, computed by this EXECUTABLE invariant (Codex iter-2 P1-2): group every supporting row by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)`; for each origin cluster L3 designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** (the syndication root / earliest independent publication; the other members are derivative copies — genuinely-INDEPENDENT corroboration has different content and forms its OWN cluster, it does not join an existing one). `cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical origin)`; copy/derivative members are attributed for disclosure ("N copies → 1 origin") but contribute ZERO to the mass. The claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **No row-level term, no averaging, no max-over-copies.** A row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id` only because L3 flagged it a copy of that cluster's canonical origin — so it adds NOTHING to the mass **even if its own `authority_score` is higher than the canonical origin's** (a high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content would form a new cluster). **Binding invariant (mathematically closed, Codex iter-3):** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY authority of the copier, whenever the added row joins an existing origin cluster. This requires **L3 to emit a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation, and membership** (NOT merely an `independent_origin_count` scalar) and L4 to emit `claim_cluster_id`, both BEFORE L5. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:8765:.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:2321:.\docs\credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md:151:- **Offline tests:** **the vax adversarial test** (N independent-LOOKING-but-copied low-cred sources vs few high-cred — naive count flips to the false majority, weight+collapse picks the high-cred side); **copy-invariance fixture (Codex iter-3): a copier whose own `authority_score` is HIGHER than the cluster's canonical origin joins that origin cluster → `weight_mass` is UNCHANGED (the copier is derivative, excluded from the cluster mass)**; drb_72 regression **(CONDITIONAL on the journal-only audit, Decision #4)** — assert weight-floor passage for drb_72 ONLY if the audit confirms journal-only was an implementation choice, not a protocol requirement; otherwise assert on a non-protocol-pinned fixture (a protocol-pinned `source_restriction: journal_only` keeps its hard T1+T2 filter); corpus_approval still blocks material-deviation auto-approve even when weight-adequate (AuthorizedSweep still required); **clinical veto test (adequacy-level, Codex iter-2)** — a clinical claim with only news/commercial support is **NOT weight-adequate** / the clinical source-type veto fires (the T1/T2 clinical-tier floor is empty), even at high weight-mass. The ABSTAIN/fringe *rendering* is asserted in Phase 7 (L6 composition), not here.

.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:11864:.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:1:# I-cred-004b (#1161) — undated-cluster copy-invariance spec decision + missing-evidence_id stable id — BRIEF for Codex
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:11865:.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:10:This is a DESIGN/SPEC decision, not a diff. You raised this at the P4 (#1153) iter-5 cap. I need your ruling on the undated-cluster policy before Phase 6 (#1155) wires `cluster_mass`.
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:11866:.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:14:`src/polaris_graph/synthesis/independence_collapse.py` `_order_key`: for an ALL-undated cluster the canonical is the LOWEST-`authority_score` member. This makes `cluster_mass = authority(canonical)`:
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:11867:.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:15:- **Inflation direction FULLY blocked** — a higher-authority copy can never become canonical, so `weight_mass(rows + higher_auth_copy) == weight_mass(rows)`. ✓ (the vax-defense property)
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:11868:.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:16:- **But NOT strict equality for a LOWER-authority copy** — adding a copy whose authority is below the current min makes it the new canonical and LOWERS the mass. The plan §148 states `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY copier authority.
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:11870:.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:20:## Why strict equality is unachievable for undated differing-authority clusters
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:11872:.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:24:For an ALL-undated cluster of near-duplicate content with DIFFERING authority, there is no date and no guaranteed-monotonic identity to mark "the original." Any member-authority-based mass function that is invariant to adding an arbitrary-authority element is either a constant (throws away the signal) or ignores the new element (requires a stable original-identity we do not have). So "strict equality for ANY copier authority" cannot hold for undated clusters without either (a) requiring dates, or (b) not collapsing differing-authority undated near-dups at all.
.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:11873:.codex\I-cred-004b\brief.md:28:**1. Undated-cluster policy = conservative MIN authority (the current behaviour), documented as deliberate.** Rationale: it blocks the only DANGEROUS direction (inflation) exactly, and a lower-authority copy LOWERING the represented authority is honest information — the same content also appearing on a less-authoritative source means the claim is not exclusive to high-authority origins, so a lower mass is arguably MORE faithful, not a bug. Amend the plan to state: strict equality holds for DATED clusters; undated clusters use conservative-min (equality for copies of authority ≥ min; safe monotonic-lowering otherwise; inflation impossible for any copier authority).

.codex\I-cred-004b\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:13867:148:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**, computed by this EXECUTABLE invariant (Codex iter-2 P1-2): group every supporting row by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)`; for each origin cluster L3 designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** (the syndication root / earliest independent publication; the other members are derivative copies — genuinely-INDEPENDENT corroboration has different content and forms its OWN cluster, it does not join an existing one). `cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical origin)`; copy/derivative members are attributed for disclosure ("N copies → 1 origin") but contribute ZERO to the mass. The claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **No row-level term, no averaging, no max-over-copies.** A row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id` only because L3 flagged it a copy of that cluster's canonical origin — so it adds NOTHING to the mass **even if its own `authority_score` is higher than the canonical origin's** (a high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content would form a new cluster). **Binding invariant (mathematically closed, Codex iter-3):** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY authority of the copier, whenever the added row joins an existing origin cluster. This requires **L3 to emit a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation, and membership** (NOT merely an `independent_origin_count` scalar) and L4 to emit `claim_cluster_id`, both BEFORE L5. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.

.codex\credibility-redesign\plan_gate_verdict_iter4.stderr.txt:186:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**, computed by this EXECUTABLE invariant (Codex iter-2 P1-2): group every supporting row by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)`; for each origin cluster L3 designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** (the syndication root / earliest independent publication; the other members are derivative copies — genuinely-INDEPENDENT corroboration has different content and forms its OWN cluster, it does not join an existing one). `cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical origin)`; copy/derivative members are attributed for disclosure ("N copies → 1 origin") but contribute ZERO to the mass. The claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **No row-level term, no averaging, no max-over-copies.** A row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id` only because L3 flagged it a copy of that cluster's canonical origin — so it adds NOTHING to the mass **even if its own `authority_score` is higher than the canonical origin's** (a high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content would form a new cluster). **Binding invariant (mathematically closed, Codex iter-3):** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` for ANY authority of the copier, whenever the added row joins an existing origin cluster. This requires **L3 to emit a stable `origin_cluster_id`, the canonical-origin designation, and membership** (NOT merely an `independent_origin_count` scalar) and L4 to emit `claim_cluster_id`, both BEFORE L5. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.
.codex\credibility-redesign\plan_gate_verdict_iter4.stderr.txt:189:- **Offline tests:** **the vax adversarial test** (N independent-LOOKING-but-copied low-cred sources vs few high-cred — naive count flips to the false majority, weight+collapse picks the high-cred side); **copy-invariance fixture (Codex iter-3): a copier whose own `authority_score` is HIGHER than the cluster's canonical origin joins that origin cluster → `weight_mass` is UNCHANGED (the copier is derivative, excluded from the cluster mass)**; drb_72 regression **(CONDITIONAL on the journal-only audit, Decision #4)** — assert weight-floor passage for drb_72 ONLY if the audit confirms journal-only was an implementation choice, not a protocol requirement; otherwise assert on a non-protocol-pinned fixture (a protocol-pinned `source_restriction: journal_only` keeps its hard T1+T2 filter); corpus_approval still blocks material-deviation auto-approve even when weight-adequate (AuthorizedSweep still required); **clinical veto test (adequacy-level, Codex iter-2)** — a clinical claim with only news/commercial support is **NOT weight-adequate** / the clinical source-type veto fires (the T1/T2 clinical-tier floor is empty), even at high weight-mass. The ABSTAIN/fringe *rendering* is asserted in Phase 7 (L6 composition), not here.

.codex\credibility-redesign\plan_gate_verdict_iter3.stderr.txt:1906:.\docs\credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md:149:- **Scope:** Replace COUNT aggregation with **origin-cluster weight-mass**, computed by this EXECUTABLE invariant (Codex iter-2 P1-2): group every supporting row by `(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)`; for each origin cluster compute exactly ONE `cluster_mass = max(authority_score)` over its members; the claim-side weight-mass = Σ of `cluster_mass`, once per origin cluster within the claim cluster. **There is NO row-level term and NO averaging** — a copied / non-independent row joins an existing `origin_cluster_id`, so it changes neither the set of clusters nor their `max`, and therefore CANNOT change the mass. **Binding invariant:** `weight_mass(rows + copied_row) == weight_mass(rows)` whenever the copy joins an existing origin cluster. Without it, copied rows re-inflate the false majority and the vax test fails. Applies in plan-sufficiency AND journal-only adequacy. Per-facet weighting preserved.

1:"""Phase 4a (L3, §6c-4) — independence-collapse by content near-duplication.
2:
3:Credibility-weighted sourcing redesign, plan
4:``docs/credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md`` Phase 4a.
5:
6:WHAT THIS DOES
7:==============
8:Given a list of generator-visible evidence rows, cluster the near-DUPLICATE
9:content copies (syndication / press-release rehash / verbatim republication)
10:into ``origin clusters`` so a later weighted tally (L5, Phase 6) counts each
11:independent ORIGIN exactly once and cannot be inflated by copies.
12:
13:The collapse is layered on the existing host-collapse primitive
14:(``authority.corroboration``: ``count_independent_hosts`` /
15:``registrable_domain``). Two rows join the same origin cluster when EITHER
16:
17:  1. they share a registrable domain (eTLD+1 host-collapse — the existing
18:     primitive; subdomains of one institution are the same origin), OR
19:  2. their text content is a near-duplicate: TF-IDF cosine >= the configured
20:     similarity threshold (default ``DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD`` = 0.85),
21:
22:EXCEPT a curated acceptable-mirror allowlist (arXiv / SSRN / PMC by default)
23:is NOT collapsed on content alone across DIFFERENT registrable domains — a
24:legitimate mirror of the same paper on two scholarly hosts stays two
25:independent origins rather than being falsely flagged a copy (the
26:echo-collapse false-positive bound, plan §6 RISKS).
27:
28:CANONICAL-ORIGIN INVARIANT (the load-bearing safety property)
29:=============================================================
30:Each cluster designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** — the earliest /
31:seed member — using ONLY an order signal: an explicit publication-date key
32:when present and parseable, else the corpus order (lowest input index).
33:The per-row ``authority_score`` is DELIBERATELY NOT consulted for the
34:canonical choice or for cluster membership.
35:
36:Therefore: adding a copied row to an existing cluster — **even a copy whose
37:own ``authority_score`` is HIGHER than the cluster's canonical origin** —
38:does NOT change the cluster set nor its canonical origin. A high-authority
39:verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content
40:would form a new cluster. This is exactly what lets the L5 weighted tally
41:(``cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical_origin)``, copies contribute
42:zero) be uninflatable by copies. The invariant is proven by
43:``test_independence_collapse.py``.
44:
45:OUTPUT (per the Phase-4a emitted contract)
46:==========================================
47:``collapse_independent_origins`` returns an :class:`IndependenceCollapseResult`
48:carrying, for every input row (by index):
49:  - a stable ``origin_cluster_id`` (deterministic, derived from the canonical
50:    member so it is stable under copy-additions),
51:  - the canonical-origin row index for that cluster,
52:  - an ``is_canonical_origin`` / ``is_derivative_copy`` flag,
53:and an ``independent_origin_count`` = number of distinct origin clusters.
54:
55:PURITY / SAFETY
56:===============
57:Pure: constructs no client, opens no network, loads no model, calls no LLM,
58:mutates none of the caller's rows (membership/flags are emitted in a fresh
59:structure). snake_case, explicit imports, no host/TLD literals in code (the
60:PSL ``gov_suffixes`` and the mirror allowlist are passed in by the caller).
61:
62:DEFAULT-OFF: this module is inert library code. Nothing in the production
63:pipeline imports or invokes it; it acts ONLY when a flagged caller calls
64:``collapse_independent_origins``. It touches NO faithfulness gate
65:(strict_verify / 4-role / two-family / corpus_approval are untouched) and
66:NEVER drops a source — it only annotates duplicated corroboration.
67:"""

80:# Cosine-similarity threshold at/above which two texts are a content copy.
170:            return value
171:    return ""
