174:def _row_domain(row: dict[str, Any], gov_suffixes: tuple[str, ...]) -> str:
175:    """Registrable domain (eTLD+1) for a row's ``source_url``; ``""`` if none."""
176:    return registrable_domain(_host_of(str(row.get("source_url", ""))), gov_suffixes)
177:
178:
179:def _parse_order_date(value: Any) -> tuple[int, int, int] | None:
180:    """Parse a publication-order value to a sortable ``(year, month, day)``, or ``None``
181:    when it is not CONFIDENTLY parseable (then the row is treated as undated and sorts
182:    last). Only UNAMBIGUOUS year-first formats are accepted — ISO ``YYYY-MM-DD`` /
183:    ``YYYY/MM/DD`` / ``YYYY-MM`` / ``YYYY``, or an int/float year. Ambiguous or malformed
184:    values (e.g. ``01/01/2099`` day-first, or free text) are REJECTED so they cannot
185:    lexically outrank a real ISO date and steal the canonical origin (Codex iter-2 P1).
186:    """
187:    if isinstance(value, bool):
188:        return None
189:    if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
190:        year = int(value)
191:        return (year, 1, 1) if 1000 <= year <= 9999 else None
192:    if not isinstance(value, str):
193:        return None
194:    match = re.match(r"^(\d{4})(?:[-/](\d{1,2})(?:[-/](\d{1,2}))?)?$", value.strip())
195:    if not match:
196:        return None
197:    year = int(match.group(1))
198:    month = int(match.group(2) or 1)
199:    day = int(match.group(3) or 1)
200:    try:
201:        # REAL calendar validation — an ISO-shaped but non-calendar date (e.g. 2024-02-31,
202:        # Feb has no 31st; or 2024-13-01) raises and is treated as undated, so it cannot
203:        # outrank a genuine date and steal the canonical origin (Codex iter-3 P1).
204:        datetime.date(year, month, day)
205:    except ValueError:
206:        return None
207:    return (year, month, day)
208:
209:
210:def _order_key(row: dict[str, Any], row_index: int) -> tuple:
211:    """Deterministic canonical-selection order key (canonical = ``min`` over members).
212:
213:    A DATED row sorts before any undated row; among dated rows the earliest CALENDAR date
214:    wins and ``authority_score`` is NEVER consulted (so a higher-authority but later-dated
215:    copy can never steal the canonical origin). Same-date ties break on the STABLE
216:    evidence_id, then the input index. Codex iter-1/2/3 fixes: dated-beats-undated;
217:    malformed/non-ISO and non-calendar dates are treated as undated.
218:
219:    When EVERY member of a cluster is undated there is no date to identify the seed
220:    positionally, so the canonical is the LOWEST-``authority_score`` member: a higher-
221:    authority copy can then never become the canonical origin and inflate ``cluster_mass``
222:    (the load-bearing copy-invariance invariant — Codex iter-4 P1). The evidence_id breaks
223:    authority ties (NOT the input index), so a prepended copy cannot win on position. A
224:    later-added LOWER-authority copy may relabel the cluster to itself, but that only ever
225:    LOWERS the mass — monotonically non-increasing under additions, never inflatable.
226:
227:    Returns a 5-tuple ``(has_no_date, (y, m, d), auth_rank, evidence_id, row_index)``; the
228:    ``auth_rank`` slot is a constant ``0.0`` for DATED rows (date decides) and the real
229:    authority for undated rows (lowest wins).
230:    """
395:                uf.union(i, j)
396:
397:    # Group members by union-find root.
398:    members_by_root: dict[int, list[int]] = {}
399:    for i in range(n):
400:        members_by_root.setdefault(uf.find(i), []).append(i)
401:
402:    clusters: list[OriginCluster] = []
403:    canonical_of: dict[int, int] = {}      # member index -> canonical index
404:    cluster_id_of: dict[int, str] = {}     # member index -> origin_cluster_id
405:
406:    for member_indices in members_by_root.values():
407:        member_indices = sorted(member_indices)
408:        # Canonical = earliest/seed by the order key (authority NOT consulted).
409:        canonical_index = min(
410:            member_indices, key=lambda idx: _order_key(rows[idx], idx)
411:        )
412:        # Stable, copy-immune id: derived from the canonical row's EVIDENCE IDENTITY, NOT
413:        # its input position — so a copy added BEFORE the canonical (prepended) does not
414:        # shift the index and change the id (Codex iter-2 P2). Only when the canonical row
415:        # carries no evidence_id do we fall back to the index.
416:        canonical_eid = str(rows[canonical_index].get("evidence_id", "") or "").strip()
417:        origin_cluster_id = (
418:            f"origin::{canonical_eid}" if canonical_eid else f"origin::idx{canonical_index}"
419:        )
420:        copy_indices = [i for i in member_indices if i != canonical_index]
421:        member_hosts = sorted({domains[i] for i in member_indices} - {""})
422:        clusters.append(
423:            OriginCluster(
424:                origin_cluster_id=origin_cluster_id,
425:                canonical_index=canonical_index,

46:+Given a list of generator-visible evidence rows, cluster the near-DUPLICATE
47:+content copies (syndication / press-release rehash / verbatim republication)

62:+legitimate mirror of the same paper on two scholarly hosts stays two
63:+independent origins rather than being falsely flagged a copy (the
64:+echo-collapse false-positive bound, plan §6 RISKS).
65:+
66:+CANONICAL-ORIGIN INVARIANT (the load-bearing safety property)
67:+=============================================================
68:+Each cluster designates exactly ONE **canonical origin** — the earliest /
69:+seed member — using ONLY an order signal: an explicit publication-date key
70:+when present and parseable, else the corpus order (lowest input index).
71:+The per-row ``authority_score`` is DELIBERATELY NOT consulted for the
72:+canonical choice or for cluster membership.
73:+
74:+Therefore: adding a copied row to an existing cluster — **even a copy whose
75:+own ``authority_score`` is HIGHER than the cluster's canonical origin** —
76:+does NOT change the cluster set nor its canonical origin. A high-authority
77:+verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own *independent* content
78:+would form a new cluster. This is exactly what lets the L5 weighted tally
79:+(``cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical_origin)``, copies contribute
80:+zero) be uninflatable by copies. The invariant is proven by

codex

50:
51:def _row(
52:    evidence_id: str,
53:    url: str,
54:    text: str,
55:    *,
56:    authority_score: float | None = None,
57:    **extra: Any,
58:) -> dict[str, Any]:
59:    row: dict[str, Any] = {
60:        "evidence_id": evidence_id,
61:        "source_url": url,
62:        "direct_quote": text,
63:    }
64:    if authority_score is not None:
65:        row["authority_score"] = authority_score
66:    row.update(extra)
67:    return row
68:
69:
70:def _collapse(rows: list[dict[str, Any]], **kw: Any):
71:    return collapse_independent_origins(rows, gov_suffixes=_GOV_SUFFIXES, **kw)

247:def test_added_high_authority_copy_does_not_change_cluster_set_or_canonical() -> None:
248:    """Load-bearing copy-invariance proof.
249:
250:    Baseline: one canonical origin (low authority) + one copy.
251:    Then add ANOTHER copy whose own authority_score is far HIGHER than the
252:    canonical's. The cluster set and the canonical origin MUST be unchanged,
253:    so a weight tally over canonical origins cannot be inflated by the copy.
254:    """
255:    base = [
256:        _row("origin", "https://wire.com/release", _PRESS_RELEASE,
257:             authority_score=0.20),
258:        _row("copy1", "https://reprint1.com/s", _SYNDICATED_COPY,
259:             authority_score=0.30),
260:    ]

 succeeded in 5358ms:
261:    base_result = _collapse(base)
262:    assert base_result.independent_origin_count == 1
263:    base_cluster = base_result.clusters[0]
264:    base_canonical_eid = base[base_cluster.canonical_index]["evidence_id"]
265:    assert base_canonical_eid == "origin"
266:
267:    # Add a HIGHER-authority verbatim republisher.
268:    with_high_authority_copy = base + [
269:        _row("copy2_high_auth", "https://bigsite.com/s", _SYNDICATED_COPY,
270:             authority_score=0.99),
271:    ]
272:    result = _collapse(with_high_authority_copy)
273:
274:    # Still exactly ONE independent origin.
275:    assert result.independent_origin_count == 1
276:    cluster = result.clusters[0]
277:    # Canonical origin is STILL the original low-authority seed.
278:    assert with_high_authority_copy[cluster.canonical_index]["evidence_id"] == "origin"
279:    # The high-authority copier is flagged derivative, not canonical.
280:    high = next(
281:        a for a in result.assignments
282:        if with_high_authority_copy[a.row_index]["evidence_id"] == "copy2_high_auth"
283:    )
284:    assert high.is_derivative_copy is True
285:    assert high.is_canonical_origin is False
286:    # The stable origin_cluster_id is UNCHANGED by the copy addition.
287:    assert cluster.origin_cluster_id == base_cluster.origin_cluster_id

290:def test_canonical_never_chosen_by_authority() -> None:
291:    """Even if a derivative copy has the single highest authority in the
292:    cluster, the canonical is the earliest/seed member, never the highest
293:    authority. Authority is not consulted for the canonical choice."""
294:    rows = [
295:        _row("seed", "https://origin.com/r", _PRESS_RELEASE,
296:             authority_score=0.10),                    # earliest, low authority
297:        _row("loud_copy", "https://loud.com/r", _SYNDICATED_COPY,
298:             authority_score=1.00),                    # later, top authority
299:    ]
300:    result = _collapse(rows)
301:    assert result.independent_origin_count == 1
302:    cluster = result.clusters[0]
303:    assert rows[cluster.canonical_index]["evidence_id"] == "seed"
304:    assert cluster.copy_indices == [1]

307:def test_undated_prepended_higher_authority_copy_cannot_steal_canonical() -> None:
308:    """Codex iter-4 P1 regression: when EVERY member is undated, the canonical is the
309:    LOWEST-authority member (not the first by input index), so an undated higher-authority
310:    copy PREPENDED before the seed cannot become canonical or inflate cluster_mass, and the
311:    origin_cluster_id stays stable."""
312:    seed = _row("seed", "https://origin.com/r", _PRESS_RELEASE, authority_score=0.10)
313:    base_id = _collapse([seed]).clusters[0].origin_cluster_id
314:    high_copy = _row("loud", "https://loud.com/r", _SYNDICATED_COPY, authority_score=0.99)
315:    rows = [high_copy, seed]  # PREPEND the undated higher-authority copy before the seed
316:    result = _collapse(rows)
317:    assert result.independent_origin_count == 1
318:    cluster = result.clusters[0]
319:    # Canonical is the lowest-authority seed, NOT the prepended high-authority copy.
320:    assert rows[cluster.canonical_index]["evidence_id"] == "seed"
321:    assert cluster.origin_cluster_id == base_id
322:    loud = next(a for a in result.assignments if rows[a.row_index]["evidence_id"] == "loud")
323:    assert loud.is_derivative_copy is True

326:def test_undated_high_authority_copy_cannot_outrank_dated_canonical() -> None:
327:    """Codex iter-1 P1 regression: a DATED canonical origin + an UNDATED copy
328:    whose own authority is far higher. The undated copy must NOT become the
329:    canonical (an undated row can never outrank a dated origin), so the cluster
330:    mass over the canonical is uninflatable."""

391:def test_invalid_calendar_date_copy_cannot_become_canonical() -> None:

393:    no 31st) is treated as UNDATED via real datetime validation, so it cannot outrank a real
394:    2024-03-01 origin and steal canonical (it would have, since (2024,2,31) < (2024,3,1))."""

406:def test_canonical_uses_explicit_publication_order_when_present() -> None:
407:    """When a publication-date key is present, the EARLIEST date is canonical
408:    even if it appears later in corpus order — still order-based, never
409:    authority-based."""

414:        # Earlier date, appears second, lower authority -> should be canonical.
415:        _row("earlier_pub", "https://b.com/r", _SYNDICATED_COPY,
416:             authority_score=0.10, published_date="2025-01-15"),
417:    ]

424:def test_copy_addition_is_order_independent_for_canonical() -> None:
425:    """Re-ordering the copies in the input does not change which row is
426:    canonical (the seed) nor the cluster set."""
427:    seed = _row("seed", "https://origin.com/r", _PRESS_RELEASE,
428:                authority_score=0.10, published_date="2024-01-01")
429:    copy_a = _row("ca", "https://a.com/r", _SYNDICATED_COPY,
430:                  authority_score=0.95, published_date="2024-05-01")
431:    copy_b = _row("cb", "https://b.com/r", _SYNDICATED_COPY,
432:                  authority_score=0.99, published_date="2024-09-01")
433:    r1 = _collapse([seed, copy_a, copy_b])
434:    r2 = _collapse([copy_b, seed, copy_a])
435:    assert r1.independent_origin_count == r2.independent_origin_count == 1
436:    assert r1.clusters[0].member_hosts == r2.clusters[0].member_hosts
437:    # Canonical is the seed in both orderings.
438:    assert (
439:        [seed, copy_a, copy_b][r1.clusters[0].canonical_index]["evidence_id"]
440:        == "seed"
441:    )
442:    assert (
443:        [copy_b, seed, copy_a][r2.clusters[0].canonical_index]["evidence_id"]
444:        == "seed"
445:    )

448:# ── Emitted-contract groupability (Phase 6 can group by origin_cluster_id) ──

450:
451:def test_origin_cluster_id_is_stable_and_groupable() -> None:
452:    """Every row carries a stable origin_cluster_id; grouping assignments by
453:    it reproduces the cluster membership (what Phase 6's weight-mass needs)."""
454:    rows = [
455:        _row("e0", "https://wire.com/r", _PRESS_RELEASE),
456:        _row("e1", "https://reprint.com/r", _SYNDICATED_COPY),     # copy of e0
457:        _row("e2", "https://univ.edu/study", _INDEPENDENT_FINDING),
458:    ]
459:    result = _collapse(rows)
460:    grouped: dict[str, list[int]] = {}
461:    for a in result.assignments:
462:        grouped.setdefault(a.origin_cluster_id, []).append(a.row_index)
463:    # Exactly one id per cluster; membership matches the cluster objects.
464:    assert len(grouped) == result.independent_origin_count
465:    for cluster in result.clusters:
466:        assert sorted(grouped[cluster.origin_cluster_id]) == sorted(
467:            cluster.member_indices
468:        )
469:    # Each cluster has exactly one canonical row.
470:    for cluster in result.clusters:
471:        canonicals = [
472:            a for a in result.assignments
473:            if a.origin_cluster_id == cluster.origin_cluster_id
474:            and a.is_canonical_origin
475:        ]
476:        assert len(canonicals) == 1
477:        assert canonicals[0].row_index == cluster.canonical_index

483:def test_threshold_is_caller_configurable() -> None:
484:    """Raising the threshold above the pair's cosine prevents collapse;
485:    proves the 0.85 default is config, not a hardcoded magic gate."""
486:    rows = [
487:        _row("e0", "https://a.com/r", _PRESS_RELEASE),
488:        _row("e1", "https://b.com/r", _SYNDICATED_COPY),
489:    ]
490:    collapsed = _collapse(rows, similarity_threshold=0.85)
491:    assert collapsed.independent_origin_count == 1
492:    # An impossibly high threshold disables content-collapse entirely.
493:    not_collapsed = _collapse(rows, similarity_threshold=1.0001)
494:    assert not_collapsed.independent_origin_count == 2
495:    assert not_collapsed.similarity_threshold == 1.0001
